text:
Iran is making another push to direct all Hormuz traffic to its shipping lanes, away from the Omani side. It has fired to several vessels this morning. 

Part of its plan seems, too, allowing multiple Japanese ships to cross: yesterday 6 Japanese oil tankers crossed, and 2 more are crossing today.
quoted_text:
۰۴:۱۳/ ۱۶ تیر
فشار نظامی ایران برای انحصار عبور از تنگه هرمز به مسیر تعیین شده توسط ایران ادامه دارد و تشدید شده است. مطابق یادداشت تفاهم تعهد ایران صرفا باز کردن تنگه هرمز با اعمال ترتیبات ایرانی است که اکنون اعمال این ترتیبات از جمله تعیین مسیر در جریان است. ایران هیچ نوع مصالحه درباره تنگه هرمز را نخواهد پذیرفت.

text: @gbrew24 They have already emerged on the other side; that location is several hours old.

Iran has attacked at least two (and probably tree) oil/LNG tankers in the last 24 hours in the Strait of Hormuz. If history serves of any guide, the US is likely to retaliate soon against Tehran. All SoH traffic has gone, again, dark, with vessels switching off their AIS.

While Iran attacked today at least 3 oil and LNG tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, it was rather busy loading its own tankers at Kharg Island. Activity in the major Iranian oil terminal has picked up significantly from previous weeks. 📷 @CopernicusEU Sentinel-2 🛰️ https://t.co/UVMaAPli48
BREAKING: US revokes the sanctions waiver on Iran’s oil and petrochemical sales after Tehran attacked several tankers earlier today in the Strait of Hormuz https://t.co/fhJKO8lJD8
BREAKING: US military says it’s “launching a series of powerful strikes against Iran to impose heavy costs for targeting and attacking commercial shipping.”
quoted_text: Iran has attacked at least two (and probably tree) oil/LNG tankers in the last 24 hours in the Strait of Hormuz. If history serves of any guide, the US is likely to retaliate soon against Tehran. All SoH traffic has gone, again, dark, with vessels switching off their AIS.
